title: Podman pull local image from docker-daemon failing: docker-daemon transport unsupported error.
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to move out of Docker desktop and moving existing images from docker-daemon to Podman. The ```podman pull docker-daemon:<image>:<tag>``` is failing. 
NOTE: Podman is in high demand as the Docker desktop free usage gets over in a weeks time (31st Jan 2022)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Setup  podman using the ```brew  install podman```

2. Run
```
podman machine init
podman machine start
```
3. Run
```
podman pull docker-daemon:splunk/splunk:8.2.1-a2
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
➜  ~ podman --log-level debug pull docker-daemon:splunk/splunk:8.2.1-a2
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Called pull.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug pull docker-daemon:splunk/splunk:8.2.1-a2)
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/########/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:########### ssh-ed25519
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.######/Listeners", ssh-agent signer(s) enabled
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v3.4.4/libpod/_ping
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
DEBU[0000] No credentials for #######.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com found
DEBU[0000] No credentials for #######.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com found
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v3.4.4/libpod/images/pull
Error: unsupported transport docker-daemon in "docker-daemon:splunk/splunk:8.2.1-a2": only docker transport is supported
```
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
➜  ~ podman -v
podman version 3.4.4
```
**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
macOS Big Sur v11.5.2
```
➜  ~ uname -a
 20.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Wed Jun 23 00:26:31 PDT 2021; root:xnu-7195.141.2~5/RELEASE_X86_64 x86_64

➜  ~ cat /etc/containers/registries.conf
cat: /etc/containers/registries.conf: No such file or directory

➜  ~ docker images | grep splunk
splunk/splunk                                                         8.2.1-a2                          47b86c8c0884   5 months ago    1.93GB
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out. The remote client as on Mac OS can only pull from the `docker://` transport.

Only the native Linux clients supports the transports mentioned in `man containers-transports`.
